title: Verilator seg fault on openhwgroup/cvw
body:
Thanks for taking the time to report this.

Can you attach an example that shows the issue?  (Must be openly licensed, ideally in test_regress format.)

```
harris@chips:~/repos/cvwt$ git clone https://github.com/openhwgroup/cvw
Cloning into 'cvw'...
...
edit cvw/testbench/testbench.sv and change line 38 to 
  parameter TEST="arch64i";
harris@chips:~/repos/cvwt$ cd cvw/sim
harris@chips:~/repos/cvwt/cvw/sim$ ./verilate 
rv64gc simulating...
make: Entering directory '/home/harris/repos/cvwt/cvw/sim/obj_dir'
ccache g++ -Os  -I.  -MMD -I/usr/local/share/verilator/include -I/usr/local/share/verilator/include/vltstd -DVM_COVERAGE=0 -DVM_SC=0 -DVM_TRACE=0 -DVM_TRACE_FST=0 -DVM_TRACE_VCD=0 -faligned-new -fcf-protection=none -Wno-bool-operation -Wno-overloaded-virtual -Wno-shadow -Wno-sign-compare -Wno-uninitialized -Wno-unused-but-set-parameter -Wno-unused-but-set-variable -Wno-unused-parameter -Wno-unused-variable     -DVL_TIME_CONTEXT   -fcoroutines -c -o verilated.o /usr/local/share/verilator/include/verilated.cpp
...
Archive ar -rcs Vtestbench__ALL.a Vtestbench.o Vtestbench___024root__DepSet_hfc24d085__0.o Vtestbench___024root__DepSet_hfc24d085__1.o Vtestbench___024root__DepSet_hfc24d085__2.o Vtestbench___024root__DepSet_hfc24d085__3.o Vtestbench___024root__DepSet_hfc24d085__4.o Vtestbench___024root__DepSet_hed41eec4__0.o Vtestbench___024root__DepSet_hed41eec4__1.o Vtestbench___024root__DepSet_hed41eec4__2.o Vtestbench___024root__DepSet_hed41eec4__3.o Vtestbench___024root__DepSet_hed41eec4__4.o Vtestbench___024root__DepSet_hed41eec4__5.o Vtestbench___024root__DepSet_hed41eec4__6.o Vtestbench_cacheway__pi6__DepSet_hfd209fcc__0.o Vtestbench_cacheway__pi6__DepSet_hec3e220d__0.o Vtestbench_tlbram__Pz1_T20__DepSet_h3df7cb71__0.o Vtestbench_tlbram__Pz1_T20__DepSet_hab70f5b0__0.o Vtestbench_tlbcamline__Pz1_K34_S9__DepSet_ha51865b9__0.o Vtestbench_tlbcamline__Pz1_K34_S9__DepSet_ha51865b9__1.o Vtestbench_tlbcamline__Pz1_K34_S9__DepSet_h14165bf8__0.o Vtestbench__main.o Vtestbench__ConstPool_0.o Vtestbench___024root__Slow.o Vtestbench___024root__DepSet_hfc24d085__0__Slow.o Vtestbench___024root__DepSet_hfc24d085__1__Slow.o Vtestbench___024root__DepSet_hed41eec4__0__Slow.o Vtestbench___024root__DepSet_hed41eec4__1__Slow.o Vtestbench___024root__DepSet_hed41eec4__2__Slow.o Vtestbench___024root__DepSet_hed41eec4__3__Slow.o Vtestbench___024root__DepSet_hed41eec4__4__Slow.o Vtestbench___024unit__Slow.o Vtestbench___024unit__DepSet_hf87c9ffd__0__Slow.o Vtestbench_cacheway__pi6__Slow.o Vtestbench_cacheway__pi6__DepSet_hec3e220d__0__Slow.o Vtestbench_tlbram__Pz1_T20__Slow.o Vtestbench_tlbram__Pz1_T20__DepSet_h3df7cb71__0__Slow.o Vtestbench_tlbram__Pz1_T20__DepSet_hab70f5b0__0__Slow.o Vtestbench_tlbcamline__Pz1_K34_S9__Slow.o Vtestbench_tlbcamline__Pz1_K34_S9__DepSet_h14165bf8__0__Slow.o Vtestbench__Syms.o
g++ -fuse-ld=mold    verilated.o verilated_timing.o verilated_threads.o Vtestbench__ALL.a    -pthread -lpthread -latomic   -o Vtestbench
rm Vtestbench__ALL.verilator_deplist.tmp
make: Leaving directory '/home/harris/repos/cvwt/cvw/sim/obj_dir'
TEST is arch64i
./verilate: line 9: 1607893 Segmentation fault      (core dumped) ./obj_dir/Vtestbench
Verilation complete
```

What 'verilator' command line do we use to run your example?
see above

What 'verilator --version' are you using?  Did you try it with the git master version?
harris@chips:~/repos/cvwt/cvw/sim$ verilator --version
Verilator 5.019 devel rev v5.018-101-g54ad04b6e

What OS and distribution are you using?
Ubuntu 22.04LTS

May we assist you in trying to fix this in Verilator yourself?
Yes. We are trying to bring up our OpenHWGroup CORE-V Wally (cvw) core in Verilator.  It presently simulates in Questa and lints in Verilator.  It now compiles in Verilator, but seg faults.  Adding $display statements show that the crash occurs after the second rising edge of the clock (10 ticks into simulation) but before reading a memory file into the RAM.  I'm over my head on how to debug Verilator, but would be delighted to collaborate getting our core to work with Verilator.  I expect this may involve a combination of bug fixes in Verilator and perhaps code changes in the cvw testbench.
